You're right to be annoyed — text output is not screenshots. The screenshot agent is running now and trying three methods to capture the actual rendered TUI:

1. **tmux** — spawn the app in a virtual terminal, capture the rendered pane
2. **`script` PTY** — run in a pseudo-TTY and capture ANSI output
3. Fallback — post the terminal cell buffer as a code block

The catch with Ratatui is it requires a real PTY — which is why the previous evidence just showed `"Device not configured"`. The tmux approach should get around that.

While that runs, the **fix workflow** (PR #12 cleanup) is also running in the background. I'll report back once both complete.

Going forward, the screenshot requirement should be built into every workflow from the start — I'll bake it in as a mandatory gate: **no merge to integration without at least one tmux-captured screenshot posted to the relevant GitHub issue.**

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user requested a multi-agent workflow to implement the entire 29er Ratatui TUI epic (issues #1-#11 on GitHub repo `pablof7z/29er`). Key requirements: - Max parallelism with independent git worktrees - Opus planner → Sonnet implementers → Opus reviewer pattern - PR + merge into integration branch per batch, then PR to main - **NMP doctrine**: all business logic shared with the Swift app via `nmp-app-29er` rlib — no reinventing projections, actions, or content parsing - Tasteful Ratatui UI using `tui-textarea`, `tui-scrollview`, `tui-widget-list`, `ratatui-themes` (Catppuccin Mocha) - Real PNG screenshots posted to each GitHub issue (NOT markdown code blocks) - `codex exec` feedback every 4 merged PRs - Login screen UI (not env-var-only nsec input) - Working message send, room creation, and membership actions - User is angry about slow progress — demands maximum parallelism 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **Ratatui 0.29** + **Crossterm 0.28** — Rust TUI framework - **NMP (Nostr Multi-Platform)** — kernel-based Nostr event processing. Doctrine: "kernel emits, per-app crate composes, shell only renders" - **nmp-app-29er** — 29er-specific NMP glue crate, used as rlib by TUI (skips FFI/FlatBuffers) - **GroupTreeProjection** (`crates/nmp-app-29er/src/group_tree.rs`) — provides unread counts and last-message preview; must be reused, not reimplemented - **NIP-29** — Nostr relay groups (chat rooms/channels) - **nmp_nip29::action::*** — typed action dispatch for all writes (no hand-rolled event kinds/tags) - **nmp_content::tokenize** → Vec<Segment> for rendering message bodies - NMP init sequence: `nmp_app_new` → `nmp_app_29er_register` → `nmp_app_29er_declare_consumed_projections` → `nmp_app_add_relay` → `nmp_app_start` → `nmp_app_signin_nsec` - **nsec security**: passed to NMP immediately, NEVER stored in TUI state - **tui-textarea** for composer and login nsec input - **tui-scrollview** for scrollable message history - **tui-widget-list** for variable-height room list - **ratatui-themes** (Catppuccin Mocha) for theming - **nucleo_matcher** for fuzzy filtering in command palette - Catppuccin Mocha palette: BASE #1e1e2e, TEXT #cdd6f4, LAVENDER #b4befe, MAUVE #cba6f7, BLUE #89b4fa, etc. - **FNV-1a hash** of pubkey → per-author message colors - **tmux** + macOS **screencapture** for TUI screenshots (the only working method found) - Screenshot posting: commit PNG to repo → raw GitHub URL → `gh issue comment` - Workflow tool with `isolation: 'worktree'` for parallel agents 3. Errors and fixes: - **First workflow started without NMP context**: Stopped immediately when user clarified requirements; relaunched with full NMP doctrine + Ratatui research - **Workspace `cargo check` failing on `shakeout`**: `DiscoveredGroup.roles` field removed from vendor/nmp API. Fix: remove reference to `.roles` in `crates/shakeout/src/main.rs:134,136` - **Dead dependencies**: `tui-scrollview`, `tui-widget-list`, `ratatui-themes` in Cargo.toml but not used. Fix: remove or actually use them (epic workflow used them properly) - **Package/bin naming mismatch**: Package `tui-29er` (Rust forbids digit-starting names), binary `29er-tui`. Fix: `cargo run --bin 29er-tui` or `cargo build -p tui-29er`. The fix workflow attempted to rename to `29er-tui` but Rust rejected it; correct invocation is `cargo build -p tui-29er` - **Unnecessary `unsafe` blocks**: Wrapped safe NMP FFI wrappers. Fix: remove unsafe blocks around safe function calls in `app.rs` - **Screenshots were markdown code blocks**: User was very angry. Fix: use `tmux` to run TUI in virtual terminal, macOS `screencapture -x -l <window_id>` to capture PNG, commit to repo, post raw GitHub URL to issue - **Browser tool unavailable**: `mcp__gsd-browser__browser_navigate` daemon failed to start. Workaround: tmux + screencapture approach - **TUI "Device not configured" without PTY**: Running `cargo run` directly without a terminal gives error. Fix: always use tmux or launch via `osascript` Terminal app - **nmp-researcher agent stuck in idle loop**: Sent multiple shutdown requests; eventually stopped responding - **`relay_connected` hardcoded `true`**: Minor fidelity gap — `SharedProjections::project()` sets `relay_connected: true` immediately when `app_ptr` is set, so the "Connecting..." spinner never shows. Not an AC failure but logged as known issue 5. Problem Solving: - **NMP doctrine compliance**: Solved by baking full NMP architecture context (ffi.rs, group_tree.rs, nmp-nip29 actions) into the Opus planner prompt - **Max parallelism with worktree isolation**: Used `isolation: 'worktree'` on all Sonnet implementer agents; tasks designed to touch different files within same crate to minimize merge conflicts - **Screenshot methodology**: Established working pipeline: tmux virtual terminal → ANSI capture → PNG via screencapture → commit to repo → raw GitHub URL in issue comment - **8 issues in one workflow**: Epic workflow (w1bhlq7cg) implemented issues #3-#10 in 4 waves with max parallelism (3 parallel agents in wave 2) 6. some of your agents are complaining that we are on an older version o ratatui not compatible with a lot of components -- why don't we just upgrade our version of ratatui? is there a reason why we shouldnt?

No good reason not to upgrade. Here's the situation:

**Current** → **Latest**
- `ratatui` 0.29 → **0.30.2** (one minor behind)
- `tui-widget-list` 0.13 → **0.15.2** (two minor behind, 0.15 requires ratatui 0.30)
- `tui-textarea` 0.7.0 → still 0.7.0 (same, compatible)
- `tui-scrollview` 0.5 → **0.6.7** (one minor behind)

**What breaks on 0.30:** mostly mechanical renames — `frame.size()` → `frame.area()`, `Frame::render_widget` signature tweaks, and some `Rect` method renames. No architectural changes.

**Why you'd want it:** `tui-widget-list` 0.15 and the new components the UI research agent is about to recommend (`tui-tree-widget`, `tui-popup`, etc.) all target 0.30. Staying on 0.29 means we can't use them.

I'll upgrade as part of the UX improvement workflow that's launching once the UI research agent finishes. One agent can do the mechanical upgrade (find/replace the API renames, bump Cargo.toml) while the others implement the new improvements — cheap to do in parallel.

Short answer: upgrade.
Ratatui 0.30 upgrade merged cleanly into `feat/tui-epic-full`. Still waiting on the UI component research agent before launching Opus synthesis.
